scope: Top 32 hospitalist diagnoses with deep specificity ladders + thin ICD-10 CC/MCC fallback table for everything else

global_rules.evidence_binding: Every suggested diagnosis must map to literal phrase/value in pasted note. No match = drop or move to soft-flag.
hard_rules_universal: Medications alone never confirm a diagnosis (Arixtra/Lovenox SQ ≠ DVT/PE/AFib confirmed)
Lab values alone never confirm a diagnosis (troponin elevation alone ≠ MI; BNP alone ≠ HF)
Imaging findings without clinical correlation = ask, not assert
Prior records: pulled-forward dx must be tagged with date/source, never asserted as current
Institution-specific: troponin 0–20 pg/mL is normal — never flag ≤20 as elevated
global_rules.soft_flag_principle: When evidence is suggestive but not confirmatory, surface as visible CDI suggestion with caveat — never as asserted A&P diagnosis
global_rules.cdi_specificity_principle: Permissive suggestion mode for specificity upgrades. Frame as query opportunity, never asserted in A&P.

specificity_ladder.label: AKI due to acute tubular necrosis (ATN)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: AKI documented + sepsis/hypotension/contrast/nephrotoxin without cause specified

specificity_ladder.label: AKI due to acute cortical necrosis
AKI on CKD
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Cr elevated + prior records show CKD without acute-on-chronic language

specificity_ladder.label: Cardiorenal syndrome
evidence_required: AKI temporally linked to HF or diuresis

kdigo_staging.stage_3: Cr ≥3× baseline OR ≥4.0 mg/dL OR RRT initiated
kdigo_staging.documentation_value: Stage does not change CC/MCC but supports severity

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ AKI SPECIFICITY: Cause not specified. [Sepsis/hypotension/contrast/nephrotoxin] present — consider [ATN/HRS/cardiorenal/contrast-induced] (MCC where applicable).
ai_behavior.rationale: AKI unspecified = CC. ATN, HRS, and cortical necrosis = MCC. Cause specification is the moat.

hard_rules: Never infer ATN from Cr rise alone
Never assume AKI-on-CKD without documented CKD baseline
Never code HRS without cirrhosis documented
common_misses: AKI on CKD undocumented when both present
ATN unspecified when sepsis + hypotension clearly present
Cause not specified (default to N17.9 = lower specificity)

specificity_ladder.label: Sepsis due to specified organism
evidence_required: or clinical sepsis with identified source culture
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Sepsis documented + culture positive but organism not linked to sepsis dx

specificity_ladder.label: Severe sepsis (sepsis with acute organ dysfunction)
evidence_required: AND at least one acute organ dysfunction: AKI, acute resp failure, encephalopathy, acute liver dysfunction, DIC/thrombocytopenia, lactic acidosis >2, ileus
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Sepsis + any acute organ dysfunction documented but 'severe sepsis' or explicit linkage not used
specificity_ladder.documentation_value: R65.20 requires explicit linkage — physician must state 'sepsis with X' or 'severe sepsis' for capture

specificity_ladder.label: Septic shock
evidence_required: vasopressor requirement OR persistent hypotension despite fluid resuscitation
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Sepsis + vasopressors OR MAP <65 despite fluids OR lactate >2 post-resuscitation, but 'septic shock' not used
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Never code septic shock from hypotension alone without sepsis documented

specificity_ladder.label: Sepsis with specified source
evidence_required: source identified: pneumonia, UTI, cellulitis, intra-abdominal, CLABSI, etc.
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Sepsis documented but source not stated when imaging/cultures/exam point to source
specificity_ladder.documentation_value: 'Urosepsis' alone is non-specific — requires 'sepsis due to UTI' linkage

specificity_ladder.label: Bacteremia (NOT sepsis)
evidence_required: positive blood culture WITHOUT clinical sepsis criteria
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Bacteremia ≠ sepsis. Do not upgrade without SIRS/Sepsis-3 criteria
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Positive blood culture + clinical sepsis criteria present but only 'bacteremia' written

specificity_ladder.label: SIRS due to non-infectious cause
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Do not code as sepsis if no infection source

specificity_ladder.label: Post-procedural sepsis
evidence_required: sepsis temporally linked to recent procedure/surgery
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Sepsis + recent procedure but procedural linkage not documented

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ LINKAGE OPPORTUNITY: [organ dysfunction] documented with sepsis but not explicitly linked. Consider stating 'sepsis with acute [X] due to sepsis' to capture severe sepsis (R65.20, MCC).
ai_behavior.rationale: Severe sepsis (R65.20) and septic shock (R65.21) capture depends entirely on physician linkage language. Highest-yield CDI opportunity in hospital medicine.

hard_rules: Never code sepsis from positive blood culture alone — requires clinical sepsis criteria
Never code septic shock without sepsis + hemodynamic criteria
Never code 'urosepsis' as sepsis — requires explicit 'sepsis due to UTI' language
Severe sepsis (R65.20) requires explicit linkage of organ dysfunction to sepsis
SIRS without infection ≠ sepsis
common_misses: Sepsis with AKI/encephalopathy/resp failure documented but not linked → severe sepsis (R65.20) lost
Vasopressor use without 'septic shock' language → septic shock (R65.21) lost
Organism identified in cultures but sepsis remains 'unspecified'
Source not specified
'Urosepsis' written instead of 'sepsis due to UTI'
documentation_pearls: Use explicit linkage: 'sepsis with acute encephalopathy due to sepsis' not just 'sepsis. encephalopathy.'
If vasopressors or persistent hypotension → state 'septic shock'
If organism known → name it: 'sepsis due to E. coli'

specificity_ladder.label: Acute systolic (HFrEF) heart failure
evidence_required: acute decompensation: dyspnea, weight gain, edema, elevated BNP, IV diuresis
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: HF + EF ≤40% + acute symptoms but acuity/type not specified

specificity_ladder.label: Acute on chronic systolic (HFrEF) heart failure
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Prior records show HFrEF + current acute symptoms but only 'CHF exacerbation' written

specificity_ladder.label: Acute diastolic (HFpEF) heart failure
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: HF + preserved EF + acute symptoms but 'diastolic'/'HFpEF' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Acute on chronic diastolic (HFpEF) heart failure
Acute combined systolic and diastolic (HFmrEF) heart failure
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: EF 41–49% + acute HF — consider HFmrEF specificity

specificity_ladder.label: Acute on chronic combined heart failure
Right heart failure, acute
evidence_required: often pulmonary HTN, PE, RV infarct
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: RV dysfunction + acute symptoms but 'right heart failure' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Acute on chronic right heart failure
High-output heart failure
evidence_required: high cardiac output state: severe anemia, hyperthyroidism, AV fistula, sepsis, beriberi

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ HF SPECIFICITY: 'CHF' alone codes to I50.9 (no CC/MCC). Specify acuity + type. EF [value] + [context] suggests [I50.XX].
ai_behavior.rationale: Unspecified HF (I50.9) captures NEITHER CC nor MCC. Every dimension matters: acuity + EF-based type + chronicity.

hard_rules: Never code 'CHF exacerbation' alone — always specify acuity + type
Never assume HFrEF without EF ≤40% documented
Never assume HFpEF without EF ≥50% + diastolic dysfunction evidence
Acute-on-chronic requires BOTH baseline HF documented AND acute decompensation
Never infer HF from BNP alone — BNP rises in AKI, sepsis, AFib without HF
Never infer HF from edema alone
common_misses: 'CHF exacerbation' → I50.9 (no CC/MCC) instead of I50.23/I50.33/I50.43 (MCC)
Acuity not specified
Type not specified
EF documented in echo but not referenced in A&P
Right heart failure not separately coded
documentation_pearls: Reference EF: 'HFrEF (EF 30%)' or 'HFpEF (EF 60%, grade II diastolic dysfunction)'
State precipitant: dietary, AFib RVR, ischemia, infection, non-adherence

medication_triggers.specificity: high — FDA-approved for HFrEF and HFpEF
medication_triggers.query_trigger: Entresto on home med list but HF not in problem list
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on Entresto — FDA-approved for HF. Query HF history and EF documentation.

medication_triggers.specificity: very high — HFrEF only (EF ≤35%, NSR, HR ≥70)
medication_triggers.query_trigger: Ivabradine on home med list but HFrEF not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on Ivabradine — indicated for HFrEF (EF ≤35%). Query HFrEF and obtain EF.

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Both hydralazine AND isosorbide on home med list but HF not documented
medication_triggers.hard_rule: Hydralazine alone is NOT specific. Both meds together = strong HF signal.
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on hydralazine + isosorbide combo — HFrEF therapy. Query HFrEF history.

specificity_ladder.label: Bacterial pneumonia, organism specified
evidence_required: clinical pneumonia + organism on culture or clinically presumed
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Pneumonia + organism on culture but not linked in dx

specificity_ladder.label: Streptococcus pneumoniae pneumonia
evidence_required: S. pneumoniae on culture or urine antigen

specificity_ladder.label: Aspiration pneumonia
evidence_required: witnessed aspiration OR risk factors (dysphagia, AMS, vomiting, NG, neuromuscular, stroke) + dependent-lobe consolidation
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Pneumonia + aspiration risk (stroke, dementia, dysphagia, ETOH, post-extubation) but 'aspiration pneumonia' not stated
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Aspiration pneumonitis (J95.4, chemical) ≠ aspiration pneumonia (J69.0, infectious)

specificity_ladder.label: Healthcare-associated/hospital-acquired pneumonia (HAP)
evidence_required: pneumonia ≥48h after admission OR within 90 days of healthcare exposure
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Pneumonia onset during admission or healthcare-exposed but HAP not specified

specificity_ladder.label: Ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Intubated + new infiltrate ≥48h but VAP not stated

specificity_ladder.label: COVID-19 pneumonia
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Sequence U07.1 first; J12.82 secondary

specificity_ladder.label: Pneumonia, unspecified organism
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Always preferable to specify organism when known

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ PNEUMONIA SPECIFICITY: [organism/aspiration risk/hospital onset/VAP context] present but pneumonia coded as unspecified. Specify to capture MCC.
ai_behavior.rationale: Unspecified pneumonia (J18.9) = CC. Most gram-negative, staph, aspiration, viral, fungal, and HAP/VAP = MCC.

hard_rules: Never assume MRSA pneumonia from MRSA nasal swab alone
Aspiration pneumonia ≠ aspiration pneumonitis
Atelectasis on imaging ≠ pneumonia without clinical correlation
Tracheobronchitis ≠ pneumonia
Colonization ≠ infection
HAP/VAP requires ≥48h hospital/ventilator exposure
common_misses: Organism on culture but coded J18.9
Aspiration risk + dependent infiltrate but coded CAP
Hospital-onset same as community-acquired
Sepsis from pneumonia not linked
documentation_pearls: Link to sepsis: 'Sepsis due to pneumonia'
State imaging if culture-negative: lobar, multilobar, cavitary, necrotizing

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Inhaled antipseudomonal on home med list + pneumonia → query Pseudomonas
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on inhaled antipseudomonal therapy. Consider Pseudomonas pneumonia.

specificity_ladder.label: COPD with acute exacerbation
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: COPD + acute symptoms + treatment escalation but 'acute exacerbation' not used

specificity_ladder.label: COPD with acute lower respiratory infection
evidence_required: COPD + LRTI: purulent sputum, fever, leukocytosis, infiltrate
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: COPD + LRTI + abx but coded as J44.1 only
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: If pneumonia present, code BOTH J44.0 AND specific pneumonia code

specificity_ladder.label: COPD exacerbation + acute respiratory failure
evidence_required: COPD exacerbation + acute resp failure criteria (PaO2 <60, SpO2 <91%, PaCO2 >50 with pH <7.35, BiPAP)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: COPD exacerbation + BiPAP OR ABG abnormal but acute resp failure not coded
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: BiPAP for COPD exacerbation = strong indicator of acute hypercapnic resp failure

specificity_ladder.label: COPD exacerbation + chronic respiratory failure
evidence_required: chronic resp failure baseline (home O2, home BiPAP for hypercapnia)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Home O2 or home BiPAP + COPD exacerbation but acute-on-chronic not specified

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ COPD MCC OPPORTUNITY: COPD exacerbation alone = CC. Acute respiratory failure linkage captures MCC. Check ABG/SpO2/O2 device.
ai_behavior.rationale: COPD exacerbation alone = CC. MCC almost always comes from linked acute respiratory failure.

hard_rules: Never code COPD exacerbation without documented COPD baseline
Outpatient inhaler prescription alone doesn't establish COPD
Bronchitis without COPD ≠ COPD exacerbation
Asthma exacerbation ≠ COPD exacerbation
BiPAP for OSA alone ≠ chronic respiratory failure
Pneumonia with COPD requires separate pneumonia code
common_misses: Acute resp failure not coded when criteria met → MCC lost
Pneumonia not separately coded
Home O2 patient not coded as acute-on-chronic resp failure
ACO not recognized
Tobacco use not coded
documentation_pearls: If LRTI: 'COPD with acute exacerbation and acute lower respiratory infection'
Always assess resp failure: 'with acute hypercapnic respiratory failure'

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Trelegy on home med list + respiratory presentation but COPD not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on Trelegy — query COPD or asthma history.

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Breztri on home med list but COPD not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on Breztri — COPD only. Query COPD history.

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Anoro or Stiolto on home med list but COPD not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: LAMA/LABA combo — COPD-specific. Query COPD.

medication_triggers.specificity: very high — severe COPD with chronic bronchitis
medication_triggers.query_trigger: Roflumilast on home med list but severe COPD not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Roflumilast — severe COPD with chronic bronchitis.

specificity_ladder.label: Mild intermittent asthma with acute exacerbation
Mild persistent asthma with acute exacerbation
Moderate persistent asthma with acute exacerbation
Severe persistent asthma with acute exacerbation
evidence_required: severe persistent (continuous, FEV1 <60%, high-dose ICS+LABA, biologics)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Asthma exac + biologic OR high-dose ICS+LABA OR frequent admissions but severity not specified

specificity_ladder.label: Status asthmaticus
evidence_required: severe asthma unresponsive to standard therapy
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Asthma exac + ICU OR intubation OR continuous nebs OR IV mag OR refractory but 'status asthmaticus' not stated
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Status asthmaticus = MCC. Severe asthma exacerbation alone (J45.51) = CC only.

specificity_ladder.label: Asthma + acute respiratory failure
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Asthma exac + BiPAP/intubation/ABG abnormal but resp failure not coded

specificity_ladder.label: Asthma-COPD overlap (ACO)
evidence_required: features of both: atopic + smoking history with chronic obstruction
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Asthma + significant smoking + chronic obstruction → consider ACO

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ ASTHMA MCC OPPORTUNITY: Asthma exacerbation alone = CC. Status asthmaticus (J45.902) OR linked acute respiratory failure captures MCC.
ai_behavior.rationale: MCC requires status asthmaticus or linked acute respiratory failure. Both common in admissions but frequently uncoded.

hard_rules: Never code asthma without documented diagnosis
COPD exacerbation ≠ asthma exacerbation
Wheeze alone ≠ asthma (HF, PE, anaphylaxis, FB, VCD wheeze)
Status asthmaticus requires refractoriness — not synonymous with severe
ICU alone doesn't establish status without refractoriness
Bronchospasm in non-asthma context ≠ asthma exacerbation
common_misses: Severe exacerbation with continuous nebs/IV mag/ICU but status asthmaticus not documented → MCC lost
Intubated asthma → resp failure not coded → MCC lost
Severity not specified → defaults to J45.901
ACO not recognized in smokers
documentation_pearls: Format: '[Severity] persistent asthma with acute exacerbation'
Document precipitant: viral URI, allergen, NSAIDs, GERD, non-adherence
Document phenotype if biologic: eosinophilic, allergic, T2-high

medication_triggers.medication: Asthma biologics (omalizumab/Xolair, mepolizumab/Nucala, benralizumab/Fasenra, dupilumab/Dupixent, tezepelumab/Tezspire, reslizumab/Cinqair)
medication_triggers.query_trigger: On asthma biologic + respiratory admission but severe persistent asthma not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: On asthma biologic — severe persistent asthma. Query J45.51 classification and phenotype.

specificity_ladder.label: Toxic encephalopathy
evidence_required: toxic exposure: opioids, benzos, anticholinergics, lithium, illicit drugs, alcohol, chemo
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: AMS + clear toxic precipitant but 'toxic encephalopathy' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Metabolic encephalopathy
evidence_required: metabolic derangement: uremia, lytes, hypercalcemia, hypoglycemia, hyperammonemia, thyroid, hypoxia, hypercapnia
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: AMS + metabolic derangement but 'metabolic encephalopathy' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Toxic-metabolic encephalopathy
evidence_required: AMS + BOTH toxic AND metabolic precipitants
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: AMS + multifactorial but only one type specified

specificity_ladder.label: Septic encephalopathy
evidence_required: AMS + sepsis documented + no other clear cause
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: AMS + sepsis but encephalopathy not named OR not linked to sepsis
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Septic encephalopathy = highest-yield linkage for severe sepsis MCC capture
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Must explicitly link: 'acute encephalopathy due to sepsis'

specificity_ladder.label: Hepatic encephalopathy
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Cirrhosis + AMS + on lactulose/rifaximin but HE not stated
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Specify grade: West Haven I-IV. III-IV = with coma (K72.91)

specificity_ladder.label: Hypertensive encephalopathy
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: AMS + BP >180/120 + neuro symptoms but 'hypertensive encephalopathy' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy (HIE)
evidence_required: cardiac arrest, severe hypoxia, prolonged hypotension
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Post-arrest with persistent AMS but HIE not documented

specificity_ladder.label: Wernicke's encephalopathy
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: AUD + AMS + thiamine ordered but Wernicke's not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Acute encephalopathy, unspecified
evidence_required: AMS without clearly identified single etiology
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Use only when cause truly unclear

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ ENCEPHALOPATHY OPPORTUNITY: AMS/confusion/delirium documented with [precipitant]. Consider 'acute [type] encephalopathy' to capture MCC. If sepsis present, link explicitly to capture severe sepsis (R65.20).
ai_behavior.rationale: Encephalopathy is the single most under-captured MCC in hospital medicine. AMS/confusion/delirium documented but encephalopathy not named.

delirium_vs_encephalopathy_distinction.rule: Delirium (F05) = CC. Encephalopathy (G93.4x, G92, etc.) = MCC.
delirium_vs_encephalopathy_distinction.clinical_overlap: Most ICU/floor delirium from medical illness IS encephalopathy
delirium_vs_encephalopathy_distinction.when_delirium: Pure behavioral/perceptual disturbance without identified medical cause — rare in admissions
delirium_vs_encephalopathy_distinction.when_encephalopathy: AMS with identifiable cause — almost always the case in hospitalized patients
delirium_vs_encephalopathy_distinction.documentation_strategy: If both apply, lead with encephalopathy: 'acute metabolic encephalopathy with delirium'

hard_rules: Never assume encephalopathy without AMS documented
Never code hepatic encephalopathy without cirrhosis or acute liver failure
Never code Wernicke's without thiamine deficiency context
Never code septic encephalopathy without sepsis
Dementia alone ≠ encephalopathy (chronic vs acute)
Post-anesthesia early confusion ≠ encephalopathy unless prolonged
common_misses: 'AMS'/'confusion'/'delirium' written instead of 'encephalopathy'
Sepsis + AMS not linked → loses both encephalopathy MCC and severe sepsis MCC
Cirrhosis on lactulose for AMS but HE not stated
Multifactorial AMS coded with single etiology
Post-arrest AMS — HIE not documented
documentation_pearls: Differentiate from baseline dementia: 'acute encephalopathy superimposed on dementia'

specificity_ladder.label: Acute respiratory failure with hypoxia
evidence_required: PaO2 <60 OR SpO2 <91% RA OR new/increased O2 requirement
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: New O2 ≥2L OR SpO2 <91% RA OR PaO2 <60 but 'acute respiratory failure' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Acute respiratory failure with hypercapnia
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: PaCO2 >50 + pH <7.35 OR BiPAP for hypercapnia but dx not documented

specificity_ladder.label: Acute respiratory failure with both hypoxia and hypercapnia
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Document both: 'acute hypoxic and hypercapnic respiratory failure'

specificity_ladder.label: Acute on chronic respiratory failure with hypoxia
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Home O2 + acute worsening but 'acute on chronic' not used

specificity_ladder.label: Acute on chronic respiratory failure with hypercapnia
evidence_required: chronic hypercapnia baseline (COPD, OHS, neuromuscular, home BiPAP)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Home BiPAP OR known CO2 retainer + acute worsening but 'acute on chronic' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Chronic respiratory failure
evidence_required: stable chronic: home O2, chronic BiPAP, no acute change

specificity_ladder.label: ARDS
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Bilateral infiltrates + severe hypoxia + intubated + clinical insult but 'ARDS' not stated
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Specify severity: mild (200–300), moderate (100–200), severe (<100)

specificity_ladder.label: Post-procedural respiratory failure
evidence_required: resp failure linked to recent procedure

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ RESP FAILURE SPECIFICITY: O2/ABG/BiPAP documented but 'acute respiratory failure' language not used. Specify acuity + hypoxic vs hypercapnic to capture MCC.
ai_behavior.rationale: One of the most under-captured MCCs. Physicians document treatment (O2, BiPAP, intubation) without naming the diagnosis.

hard_rules: Never code from O2 requirement alone — need objective criteria
Acute-on-chronic requires BOTH chronic baseline AND acute worsening
BiPAP for OSA alone (chronic, stable) ≠ respiratory failure
Never assume ARDS without Berlin criteria
Post-extubation desaturation ≠ acute resp failure unless meets criteria
common_misses: 4L NC for new dyspnea but only 'hypoxia' written
BiPAP for COPD with hypercapnia but resp failure not stated
Home O2 with acute worsening coded as just 'hypoxia'
Intubated with bilateral infiltrates from sepsis — ARDS not named
Both hypoxia and hypercapnia present but only one specified
documentation_pearls: If chronic baseline: 'Acute on chronic [type] respiratory failure'
State precipitant: PNA, COPD exacerbation, HF, PE, sepsis, aspiration
If intubated: state ARDS if Berlin criteria met, with severity

specificity_ladder.label: Severe protein-calorie malnutrition
evidence_required: meets ≥2 ASPEN/AND criteria SEVERE: weight loss >5%/1mo or >7.5%/3mo or >10%/6mo, BMI <18.5 (acute) or <16 (chronic), severe muscle/fat loss, severe edema, reduced grip strength, energy intake <50% for ≥5 days (acute) or ≥1 month (chronic/social)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Significant weight loss OR low BMI OR severe muscle wasting OR poor PO documented + RD note suggests severe but only 'malnutrition' or no malnutrition coded
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Severe malnutrition (E43) is MCC. Moderate (E44.0) is CC. Specify severity always.

specificity_ladder.label: Moderate protein-calorie malnutrition
evidence_required: meets ≥2 ASPEN/AND criteria MODERATE: weight loss 1–2%/wk or 5%/mo or 7.5%/3mo, mild-moderate muscle/fat loss, intake 50–75% for ≥1 wk (acute) or <75% for ≥1 mo

specificity_ladder.label: Cachexia
evidence_required: chronic illness (cancer, HF, COPD, CKD, AIDS)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Cachexia code links to underlying chronic disease

specificity_ladder.label: Failure to thrive (adult)
evidence_required: weight loss + functional decline + nutritional decline
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Use specific malnutrition diagnosis (E43/E44.0) when criteria met instead

specificity_ladder.label: Kwashiorkor
evidence_required: severe protein deficiency with edema, hypoalbuminemia
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Rare in US adults; use with caution and dietitian support

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ MALNUTRITION OPPORTUNITY: Weight loss/low BMI/poor intake/muscle wasting documented. RD assessment recommended. Severe malnutrition (E43) = MCC; specify severity.
ai_behavior.rationale: Malnutrition is heavily under-coded. Severe (MCC) requires explicit ASPEN/AND criteria documentation. RD note alone insufficient — physician must concur in own dx.

hard_rules: Physician must independently document malnutrition dx — cannot rely solely on RD note
Must specify severity (mild/moderate/severe)
Low albumin alone ≠ malnutrition (acute phase reactant)
Weight loss must be involuntary, not from diuresis or intentional weight loss
Obesity does not preclude malnutrition (sarcopenic obesity, recent significant loss)
ASPEN/AND criteria require ≥2 of 6 to meet
common_misses: RD documents severe malnutrition but physician doesn't agree/document → MCC lost
'Poor PO intake' written without malnutrition assessment
Cachexia not coded in cancer/CHF/COPD patients
Severity not specified → defaults to E46 (CC only)
Weight loss from cancer, HIV, CHF — cachexia not captured
documentation_pearls: Format: '[Mild/Moderate/Severe] protein-calorie malnutrition due to [underlying cause]'
State weight loss percentage and timeframe
Document interventions: enteral nutrition, supplements, RD consult

ASPEN_AND_criteria: Localized or generalized fluid accumulation masking weight loss

severe_thresholds.acute (<3 mo): weight loss >2%/1wk or >5%/1mo or >7.5%/3mo, intake ≤50% for ≥5d, severe wasting

Pressure injury.base_label: Pressure ulcer of unspecified site, unspecified stage

specificity_ladder.label: Stage 2 pressure injury
evidence_required: partial-thickness loss of dermis, shallow open ulcer or blister

specificity_ladder.label: Stage 3 pressure injury
evidence_required: full-thickness skin loss, subcutaneous fat visible, no exposed bone/tendon/muscle
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Wound description suggests stage 3 (subq fat visible) but staged lower or unstaged

specificity_ladder.label: Stage 4 pressure injury
evidence_required: full-thickness with exposed bone, tendon, or muscle

specificity_ladder.label: Unstageable pressure injury
evidence_required: full-thickness loss with eschar or slough obscuring depth
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Often becomes stage 3 or 4 once debrided

specificity_ladder.label: Deep tissue pressure injury (DTPI)
evidence_required: intact or non-intact skin with persistent non-blanchable deep red, maroon, or purple discoloration; blood-filled blister

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ PRESSURE INJURY: Stage and POA status critical. Stage 3, 4, unstageable, DTPI = MCC. POA-N (hospital-acquired) = quality measure.
ai_behavior.rationale: Pressure injury staging and POA status drive both MCC capture and PSI/HAC quality measures. POA documentation must occur within 24h of admission with skin assessment.

hard_rules: Always document POA status (Y/N/U/W) — affects HAC penalties
Must document skin assessment within 24h of admission for POA accuracy
DTPI is distinct from stage 1 — purple/maroon discoloration vs red blanchable
Mucosal pressure ulcers are NOT staged (e.g., from NG tube, ETT)
Friction/shear injuries ≠ pressure injuries (different code)
Moisture-associated skin damage (MASD) ≠ pressure injury
Diabetic foot ulcers ≠ pressure ulcers (use L97.x)
common_misses: Stage not specified → defaults to unstaged (lower capture)
POA status not documented → HAC penalty risk
DTPI documented as stage 1
Wound described in nursing notes but not coded by physician
Multiple sites — only one coded
documentation_pearls: Format: '[Stage X] pressure injury, [site]' with POA
Always document on admission skin exam
If unstageable due to eschar: state explicitly

Delirium / AMS.base_label: Delirium due to known physiological condition / Altered mental status, unspecified

specificity_ladder.label: PRIMARY MAPPING: Most 'delirium/AMS' should be coded as encephalopathy
specificity_ladder.rationale: Hospitalized patients with AMS almost always have an identifiable medical/metabolic/toxic/septic cause = encephalopathy. Encephalopathy = MCC. Delirium (F05) = CC only.

specificity_ladder.label: Delirium superimposed on dementia
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Dementia + acute confusion but 'delirium' not stated AND no encephalopathy etiology identified

specificity_ladder.label: Delirium due to known physiological condition
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Prefer encephalopathy coding when underlying cause identified — MCC instead of CC

specificity_ladder.label: Altered mental status, unspecified
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Symptom code only — should always be replaced by specific diagnosis when identified

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ DELIRIUM/AMS UPGRADE OPPORTUNITY: 'Delirium' (F05) = CC only. 'AMS' (R41.82) = neither. If underlying cause identifiable (sepsis, metabolic derangement, toxic, hepatic, hypoxic), upgrade to specific encephalopathy (MCC). See encephalopathy ladder.
ai_behavior.rationale: Delirium and AMS are placeholder codes when better diagnoses exist. The financial and clinical accuracy gap between 'delirium' (CC) and 'metabolic encephalopathy' (MCC) is large and avoidable.

hard_rules: Always attempt to identify underlying cause and code as specific encephalopathy
F05 (delirium) coexists with dementia — both can be coded
R41.82 is a symptom code — least desirable; specify
Behavioral disturbance alone without physiological cause may be psychiatric — different ladder
common_misses: 'Delirium' or 'AMS' written when encephalopathy applies → MCC lost
'Toxic-metabolic encephalopathy' opportunity missed
Septic encephalopathy linkage missed → severe sepsis (R65.20) lost too
documentation_pearls: If superimposed on dementia → state 'acute encephalopathy superimposed on dementia'
Avoid R41.82 unless truly no cause identified

DKA / HHS.base_label: Diabetes with ketoacidosis / hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state

specificity_ladder.label: Type 1 DM with ketoacidosis without coma
Type 1 DM with ketoacidosis with coma
Type 2 DM with ketoacidosis without coma
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: T2DM can have DKA, especially with SGLT2 inhibitors (euglycemic DKA)

specificity_ladder.label: Type 2 DM with ketoacidosis with coma
Type 2 DM with hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state without coma
Type 2 DM with hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state with coma
Drug-induced DM with ketoacidosis
evidence_required: DM caused by drug (steroids, immunotherapy, antipsychotics)

specificity_ladder.label: Mixed DKA/HHS
specificity_ladder.code: code both DKA and HHS for the DM type
evidence_required: features of both: significant hyperglycemia + acidosis + hyperosmolarity
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Increasingly recognized; document both

specificity_ladder.label: Euglycemic DKA
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: On SGLT2i + acidosis + ketones but glucose normal — consider euglycemic DKA
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: SGLT2i can cause euglycemic DKA — don't dismiss DKA based on normal glucose

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ DM ACUTE COMPLICATION: Specify DM type (1/2/drug-induced/secondary), specific complication (DKA/HHS/mixed/euglycemic), and coma status. All combinations = MCC.
ai_behavior.rationale: DKA and HHS are both MCC. Distinction matters for clinical care; combined DM type + complication + coma status drives precise coding.

hard_rules: Differentiate T1DM (E10.x) from T2DM (E11.x) — drug regimen alone insufficient
DKA requires anion gap acidosis + ketones + hyperglycemia (or euglycemic context)
HHS requires significant hyperosmolarity (>320) without significant acidosis
Hyperglycemia alone ≠ DKA or HHS
Always identify and code precipitant: infection, MI, non-adherence, new dx
SGLT2 inhibitor + acidosis = consider euglycemic DKA even with normal glucose
common_misses: DKA in T2DM coded as T1DM (or vice versa)
Euglycemic DKA missed in SGLT2i patients
HHS coded as DKA when no significant acidosis
Mixed DKA/HHS not recognized
Precipitant (sepsis, MI) not coded as comorbidity
documentation_pearls: State precipitant: infection, non-adherence, MI, new dx, drug-induced
Document AG, pH, HCO3, osm, ketones, glucose

specificity_ladder.label: DM with diabetic CKD
evidence_required: linkage to DM (most CKD in diabetics is diabetic)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: DM + CKD without 'diabetic CKD' or 'DM with CKD' linkage
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Linkage assumption: CKD in DM is presumed diabetic unless otherwise documented

specificity_ladder.label: DM with diabetic neuropathy
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: DM + neuropathy/numbness/gabapentin but linkage not stated

specificity_ladder.label: DM with retinopathy
evidence_required: DM + retinopathy: NPDR/PDR with/without macular edema

specificity_ladder.label: DM with foot ulcer
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Code both DM with foot ulcer AND specific ulcer location/depth

specificity_ladder.label: DM with PAD
evidence_required: DM + PAD: claudication, rest pain, ulcer, gangrene

specificity_ladder.label: DM with gastroparesis
evidence_required: DM + delayed gastric emptying confirmed or clinical

specificity_ladder.label: DM with hyperglycemia (no DKA/HHS)
evidence_required: DM with significantly elevated glucose without DKA/HHS criteria
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Glucose >300 + DM + insulin escalation but no specific complication coded

specificity_ladder.label: Long-term insulin use status
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Code Z79.4 for any insulin-dependent patient (T1DM or T2DM on insulin)

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ DM SPECIFICITY: 'DM uncomplicated' (E11.9) = no CC/MCC. Link complications (CKD, neuropathy, retinopathy, PAD, ulcer, hyper/hypoglycemia) to DM. Most complications in diabetic patients are presumed diabetic unless otherwise stated.
ai_behavior.rationale: Uncomplicated DM = no capture. Each linked complication adds CC/MCC value. Linkage between DM and complication is often missed.

hard_rules: Most CKD in diabetics is presumed diabetic CKD — linkage assumption applies
Neuropathy in diabetic without other cause is presumed diabetic neuropathy
Always specify DM type (T1, T2, secondary)
Code Z79.4 for chronic insulin use
Steroid-induced hyperglycemia in non-diabetic is E09.x (drug-induced), not E11.x
Document each complication separately with linkage
common_misses: DM + CKD without linkage → missed CC/MCC capture
Insulin-dependent T2DM not coded with Z79.4
Neuropathy not linked to DM
Foot ulcer in diabetic not coded as DM with foot ulcer
Hyperglycemia in DM not coded as E11.65 when significant
documentation_pearls: Always assess and link: CKD, neuropathy, retinopathy, PAD, ulcers, gastroparesis
If steroid- or drug-induced: use E09.x

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Insulin on home med list but Z79.4 not coded
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on long-term insulin — code Z79.4 (long-term insulin use).

specificity_ladder.label: End-stage renal disease (ESRD)
evidence_required: GFR <15 + on chronic dialysis OR transplant candidate
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: ESRD requires chronic dialysis. Code Z99.2 (dependence on renal dialysis) alongside.

specificity_ladder.label: Diabetic CKD (linked)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: DM + CKD without 'diabetic CKD' linkage
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Linkage assumption: CKD in diabetic presumed diabetic

specificity_ladder.label: Hypertensive CKD
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Linkage assumption: CKD in HTN patient presumed hypertensive (in absence of DM)

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ CKD SPECIFICITY: Specify stage (1–5/ESRD). Stage 3b+ captures CC; ESRD = MCC. Link to DM/HTN when applicable.
ai_behavior.rationale: Unspecified CKD = no capture. Stage and etiology drive CC/MCC.

hard_rules: Stage by GFR; document baseline GFR
ESRD requires chronic dialysis or transplant
Linkage assumptions apply: DM + CKD = diabetic CKD; HTN + CKD = hypertensive CKD
Acute kidney injury on chronic kidney disease — code BOTH N17.9 + N18.x
Don't code AKI when Cr returns to baseline matching CKD
common_misses: Stage not specified → N18.9 (no capture)
DM + CKD not linked
ESRD coded as Stage 5 without dialysis status
AKI on CKD coded as just CKD
Z99.2 not coded with ESRD

medication_triggers.medication: Phosphate binders (sevelamer, calcium acetate, lanthanum, ferric citrate)
medication_triggers.query_trigger: Phosphate binder on home med list but CKD stage 4+/ESRD not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: On phosphate binder — implies advanced CKD. Query stage and etiology.

medication_triggers.specificity: very high — secondary hyperparathyroidism in ESRD
medication_triggers.query_trigger: Cinacalcet on home med list but ESRD/hyperparathyroidism not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: On calcimimetic — implies ESRD with secondary hyperparathyroidism.

specificity_ladder.label: Paroxysmal atrial fibrillation
evidence_required: AFib episodes terminating spontaneously or with intervention within 7 days

specificity_ladder.label: Persistent atrial fibrillation
evidence_required: AFib lasting >7 days or requiring cardioversion

specificity_ladder.label: Permanent (chronic) atrial fibrillation
evidence_required: AFib accepted as permanent; rhythm control abandoned

specificity_ladder.label: AFib with rapid ventricular response (RVR)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: RVR is descriptive; specify AFib type

specificity_ladder.label: Lone atrial fibrillation
evidence_required: AFib without structural heart disease, age <60

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ AFIB SPECIFICITY: Specify type (paroxysmal/persistent/permanent). Unspecified AFib still captures CC, but specificity supports clinical accuracy.
ai_behavior.rationale: AFib captures CC at baseline; type specification supports clinical care and quality measures.

hard_rules: Anticoagulation alone doesn't establish AFib — requires ECG/telemetry confirmation
AFib on telemetry/ECG = documented diagnosis
Must distinguish AFib from atrial flutter (different codes)
Sinus tachycardia or SVT ≠ AFib
If converted to NSR during admission, AFib still coded if documented
common_misses: Type not specified → defaults to I48.91
Anticoagulation on home meds without AFib documented in problem list (query opportunity)
Atrial flutter coded as AFib
documentation_pearls: Format: '[Paroxysmal/Persistent/Permanent] atrial fibrillation, [with RVR if applicable]'
Document anticoagulation status and CHA2DS2-VASc when relevant
Document precipitants when applicable: hyperthyroidism, sepsis, alcohol, post-op

medication_triggers.specificity: moderate — AFib most common, also VTE
medication_triggers.query_trigger: DOAC on home med list but no AFib or VTE documented in problem list
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on DOAC — query AFib or VTE history.

medication_triggers.specificity: moderate — AFib, mechanical valve, VTE, antiphospholipid
medication_triggers.query_trigger: Warfarin on home med list + no clear indication documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Patient on warfarin — query indication (AFib/valve/VTE/APS).

examples.I21.02: STEMI other coronary artery anterior wall

specificity_ladder.label: Non-ST elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI)
evidence_required: troponin elevation above 99th percentile + clinical context (symptoms, ECG without STEMI, imaging)
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Institution-specific: troponin 0–20 pg/mL is normal — never flag ≤20 as elevated. NSTEMI requires troponin ABOVE this threshold AND clinical context.

specificity_ladder.label: Type 2 MI (demand-supply mismatch)
evidence_required: clinical context of demand-supply mismatch (sepsis, hypotension, severe anemia, tachyarrhythmia, hypoxemia)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Troponin elevation + sepsis/anemia/tachyarrhythmia/hypoxemia + no acute plaque rupture but 'Type 2 MI' not stated
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Type 2 MI is increasingly recognized — distinct from Type 1 (atherosclerotic plaque rupture)

specificity_ladder.label: Subsequent MI (within 28 days)
evidence_required: new MI within 28 days of prior MI

specificity_ladder.label: Old/healed MI
evidence_required: history of MI > 28 days, no current acute features
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Old MI is HISTORY only — not coded as acute MI

specificity_ladder.label: Demand ischemia (not MI)
evidence_required: ischemic symptoms or ECG changes WITHOUT troponin elevation meeting MI criteria
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Distinct from Type 2 MI; demand ischemia lacks troponin rise

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ MI TYPE SPECIFICITY: Specify Type 1 (STEMI/NSTEMI) vs Type 2 (demand-supply). Type 2 MI is MCC and frequently missed in sepsis/anemia/tachyarrhythmia patients with troponin rise.
ai_behavior.rationale: Type 2 MI capture is a major CDI opportunity. Troponin rise + non-coronary precipitant + no plaque rupture = Type 2 MI (MCC) not just troponemia.
ai_behavior.hard_rule: Never suggest cardiac diagnosis from troponin alone — always require clinical context and explicit physician documentation.

hard_rules: Institution-specific: troponin 0–20 pg/mL is NORMAL. Never flag ≤20 as elevated.
Troponin elevation alone ≠ MI — requires clinical context (symptoms, ECG, imaging)
NSTEMI vs Type 2 MI distinction: plaque rupture context vs demand-supply
Demand ischemia ≠ MI (no troponin rise meeting criteria)
Old MI (I25.2) is history only — never coded as acute
Subsequent MI (I22.x) within 28 days of prior MI
Always document ECG findings, troponin trend, imaging, cath results
common_misses: Type 2 MI not recognized in sepsis/anemia/tachyarrhythmia — coded as troponin elevation only
STEMI location not specified
NSTEMI vs Type 2 MI confused
Old MI coded as acute
Demand ischemia coded as MI without troponin
documentation_pearls: State precipitant for Type 2 MI: sepsis, anemia, tachyarrhythmia, hypoxemia

PE / DVT.base_label: Other pulmonary embolism without acute cor pulmonale / DVT, unspecified
PE / DVT.cc_mcc: MCC for PE; CC for DVT

specificity_ladder.label: Saddle pulmonary embolism
evidence_required: PE at bifurcation of main PA on imaging

specificity_ladder.label: PE with acute cor pulmonale
evidence_required: PE + acute RV dysfunction (echo, elevated troponin/BNP, RV strain on ECG)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: PE + RV dysfunction OR elevated troponin OR S1Q3T3 but cor pulmonale not coded

specificity_ladder.label: Massive (high-risk) PE
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Massive PE = MCC + shock — code both; consider thrombolytics

specificity_ladder.label: Submassive (intermediate-risk) PE
evidence_required: PE + RV dysfunction OR elevated cardiac biomarkers but normotensive

specificity_ladder.label: Acute DVT, lower extremity, proximal
evidence_required: DVT in iliac, femoral, popliteal vein on imaging

specificity_ladder.label: Catheter-associated DVT
evidence_required: DVT temporally linked to central line/PICC

specificity_ladder.label: Personal history of VTE
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Use only when no current acute VTE

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ VTE SPECIFICITY: For PE — assess cor pulmonale, RV strain, hemodynamics. Saddle/cor pulmonale capture = MCC. For DVT — specify location and acuity. Always identify provoked vs unprovoked.
ai_behavior.rationale: PE specificity (cor pulmonale, saddle, hemodynamics) drives MCC. DVT location matters for clinical care and quality measures.
ai_behavior.hard_rule: Anticoagulation alone never confirms PE/DVT — requires imaging confirmation.

hard_rules: Anticoagulation alone (Lovenox, Arixtra, heparin SQ for prophylaxis) NEVER infers PE/DVT diagnosis
Therapeutic anticoagulation requires confirmed VTE on imaging
Never code PE without CTPA/VQ confirmation or strong clinical case (high-risk + treatment)
Never code DVT without ultrasound or CT confirmation
Distinguish provoked (transient risk: surgery, trauma, OCP) vs unprovoked (idiopathic or persistent risk)
Personal history of VTE (Z86.718) ≠ current VTE
common_misses: PE with RV strain/elevated troponin but cor pulmonale not coded
Saddle PE not specified
Massive PE with vasopressors but shock not coded
DVT location not specified
Catheter-associated DVT not linked to line
documentation_pearls: Document hemodynamics, RV function, biomarkers for PE

specificity_ladder.label: Acute peptic ulcer with hemorrhage
evidence_required: EGD-confirmed PUD with active or recent bleeding (Forrest classification)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Specify with/without obstruction; with/without perforation

specificity_ladder.label: Esophageal varices with bleeding
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Code underlying liver disease + portal HTN

specificity_ladder.label: Acute blood loss anemia
evidence_required: acute drop in Hgb attributable to blood loss
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: GIB + Hgb drop but acute blood loss anemia not separately coded
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Always assess and code D62 alongside GIB when Hgb drops

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ GIB SPECIFICITY: Specify source (peptic ulcer, varices, diverticular, etc.) when identified. Always assess for acute blood loss anemia (D62) — separately coded.
ai_behavior.rationale: Source specification (esp. PUD, varices) drives MCC. Acute blood loss anemia is commonly missed alongside GIB.

hard_rules: Source coding requires endoscopic, imaging, or pathologic confirmation
Melena/hematemesis alone = symptom; need source dx when identified
Acute blood loss anemia (D62) is distinct from GIB and should be coded when Hgb drops
Chronic GIB + iron deficiency anemia (D50.0) is different from acute blood loss anemia (D62)
Anticoagulation use coded as adverse effect when bleeding attributable
common_misses: Source identified on EGD/colonoscopy but K92.2 used
Acute blood loss anemia (D62) not coded alongside
Varices linked to portal HTN/cirrhosis not coded
Anticoagulant-related bleeding not coded as adverse effect
documentation_pearls: Format: '[Upper/Lower] GI bleed due to [specific source]'
If endoscopy done: state findings, location, intervention (clips, banding, epinephrine)
Code acute blood loss anemia (D62) when Hgb drop attributable
If anticoagulant-related: 'GI bleed in setting of [warfarin/DOAC] therapy'
Hemodynamic status: SBP, HR, transfusion requirement

specificity_ladder.label: Cirrhosis (compensated)
specificity_ladder.code: K74.60 / K70.30 (alcoholic) / K71.7 (toxic) / K74.3 (PBC) / K74.4 (PSC) / K74.69 (other)

specificity_ladder.label: Cirrhosis with ascites
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Ascites elevates capture; document

specificity_ladder.label: Portal hypertension
evidence_required: clinical or imaging evidence: varices, splenomegaly, ascites, hepatic vein gradient

specificity_ladder.label: Acute liver failure
evidence_required: coagulopathy (INR ≥1.5), encephalopathy, no prior chronic liver disease (or acute on chronic)

specificity_ladder.label: Acute on chronic liver failure (ACLF)
evidence_required: chronic liver disease + acute decompensation with organ failure

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ CIRRHOSIS DECOMPENSATION: Specify decompensation features (ascites, HE, varices, HRS, SBP, ACLF). Multiple decompensation features each carry CC/MCC value when documented separately.
ai_behavior.rationale: Cirrhosis alone = CC. Each decompensation feature adds value: ascites, HE, varices with bleed, SBP, HRS, ACLF all = MCC. Etiology specification (alcohol, NASH, viral, autoimmune, PBC, PSC) supports clinical accuracy.

hard_rules: Cirrhosis requires imaging/biopsy/clinical confirmation — not from labs alone
Specify etiology: alcoholic, NASH/NAFLD, viral (HCV, HBV), autoimmune, PBC, PSC, hemochromatosis, Wilson, A1AT
HE requires AMS attributable to liver disease — see encephalopathy ladder
HRS requires cirrhosis + AKI unresponsive to volume + low urine sodium
SBP requires ascitic fluid PMN >250 (with or without positive culture)
Acute liver failure: no prior chronic disease + INR ≥1.5 + encephalopathy
common_misses: Decompensation features documented separately but not linked to cirrhosis
Etiology not specified
Ascites with cirrhosis but K70.31/K74.6 + R18 not coded
HE in cirrhosis on lactulose but not coded as K72.9x
SBP missed when ascitic fluid PMN >250
documentation_pearls: List each decompensation: ascites, HE grade, varices ± bleeding, HRS, SBP
Code Z94.4 if liver transplant status
Document chronic alcohol use (F10.20) if applicable

medication_triggers.specificity: very high — hepatic encephalopathy in cirrhosis
medication_triggers.query_trigger: Lactulose + rifaximin on home med list but cirrhosis or HE not documented
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: On lactulose + rifaximin — implies cirrhosis with HE history. Query.

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Spironolactone + furosemide + ascites or low albumin context
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Diuretic combo suggests cirrhotic ascites. Query.

medication_triggers.query_trigger: Non-selective BB on home med list + GI/liver context
medication_triggers.suggestion_template: ⚠ MEDICATION FLAG: Non-selective BB may indicate variceal prophylaxis. Query cirrhosis/varices.

specificity_ladder.label: Severe symptomatic hyponatremia
evidence_required: neurologic symptoms: seizures, coma, severe AMS
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Severity drives clinical care and resource use; document symptoms and treatment

specificity_ladder.label: Hypervolemic hyponatremia
evidence_required: hyponatremia + edematous state (HF, cirrhosis, nephrotic, advanced CKD)

specificity_ladder.label: Pseudohyponatremia
evidence_required: normal serum osmolality despite low Na
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Pseudohyponatremia is lab artifact — do not code as hyponatremia

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ HYPONATREMIA SPECIFICITY: Specify etiology (SIADH, hypovolemic, hypervolemic, drug-induced) and severity. Symptomatic hyponatremia treated with hypertonic = severe.
ai_behavior.rationale: Hyponatremia captures CC; etiology specification supports clinical accuracy and CDI.

hard_rules: Distinguish true vs pseudohyponatremia (osmolality)
Severe = Na <125 or neurologic symptoms
SIADH requires euvolemia + low serum osm + elevated urine osm + elevated urine Na + normal thyroid/adrenal/renal
Always assess volume status (hypo/eu/hyper)
Identify and code precipitant when possible
common_misses: Etiology not specified
SIADH not coded when criteria met
Drug-induced not linked
Hypervolemic in HF/cirrhosis not linked
documentation_pearls: Document Na value, serum osm, urine osm, urine Na
If severe + symptomatic + hypertonic given: state clearly
Identify cause: SIADH, HF, cirrhosis, drug, hypovolemia, adrenal

UTI / pyelonephritis / urosepsis.base_label: Urinary tract infection, site not specified

specificity_ladder.label: Acute pyelonephritis
evidence_required: upper tract symptoms: fever, flank pain, CVA tenderness, nausea/vomiting
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: UTI + flank pain or fever or CVA tenderness but coded as N39.0 only

specificity_ladder.label: Sepsis due to UTI (urosepsis)
evidence_required: sepsis criteria + UTI as identified source
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Sepsis + UTI but linkage 'sepsis due to UTI' not stated; OR 'urosepsis' written without sepsis criteria check
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: 'Urosepsis' alone is non-specific — must explicitly state 'sepsis due to UTI' for sepsis coding

specificity_ladder.label: Catheter-associated UTI (CAUTI)
evidence_required: UTI in patient with indwelling urinary catheter
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: CAUTI is a quality measure; document carefully

specificity_ladder.label: Complicated UTI (with structural/functional abnormality)
evidence_required: UTI in patient with stones, obstruction, neurogenic bladder, immunocompromise, pregnancy, post-procedure

specificity_ladder.label: Asymptomatic bacteriuria
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Asymptomatic bacteriuria typically should NOT be coded as UTI — bacteriuria without symptoms

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ UTI SPECIFICITY: Specify level (cystitis vs pyelonephritis). If sepsis criteria met, link explicitly: 'sepsis due to UTI' (not 'urosepsis'). 'Urosepsis' alone is non-specific.
ai_behavior.rationale: Pyelonephritis vs UTI matters clinically. Sepsis linkage is highest-yield: 'sepsis due to UTI' captures sepsis MCC + organ dysfunction linkage; 'urosepsis' alone captures neither.

hard_rules: Asymptomatic bacteriuria ≠ UTI (no treatment unless pregnant or pre-urologic procedure)
Positive urine culture alone ≠ UTI without symptoms or pyuria
'Urosepsis' is informal language — always replace with 'sepsis due to UTI'
Pyelonephritis requires upper tract findings (flank pain, fever, CVA tenderness, imaging)
CAUTI requires indwelling catheter at time of UTI onset
Distinguish complicated vs uncomplicated UTI
common_misses: 'Urosepsis' written → no sepsis MCC captured
Pyelonephritis with flank pain coded as just N39.0
Sepsis from UTI not linked to UTI
CAUTI not identified
Asymptomatic bacteriuria treated and coded as UTI (overdx)
documentation_pearls: If sepsis: 'Sepsis due to UTI' explicitly
Document organism, urinalysis, imaging if obtained

specificity_ladder.label: Erysipelas
evidence_required: sharply demarcated, raised, fiery red lesion, often face/leg, classic for strep

specificity_ladder.label: Necrotizing fasciitis
evidence_required: rapid progression, severe pain out of proportion, crepitus, gas on imaging, systemic toxicity, surgical debridement
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Cellulitis + severe pain disproportionate + systemic toxicity + surgical urgency but 'necrotizing fasciitis' not stated
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Necrotizing fasciitis is surgical emergency — clinical/surgical/imaging confirmation required

specificity_ladder.label: Pyomyositis
evidence_required: bacterial infection of skeletal muscle with abscess

specificity_ladder.label: Cellulitis with sepsis
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Cellulitis + sepsis criteria but sepsis linkage to skin not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Diabetic foot infection / osteomyelitis
evidence_required: foot ulcer in DM ± osteomyelitis confirmed on imaging or probe-to-bone

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ CELLULITIS SPECIFICITY: Specify location, complications (abscess, osteo, necrotizing), and link to sepsis when applicable.
ai_behavior.rationale: Necrotizing fasciitis, osteomyelitis, and sepsis-linked cellulitis are MCC. Standard cellulitis = CC when location specified.

hard_rules: Erythema alone ≠ cellulitis (venous stasis dermatitis, gout, contact dermatitis can mimic)
Bilateral lower extremity 'cellulitis' usually venous stasis dermatitis — not bacterial
Necrotizing fasciitis is clinical/surgical emergency — high suspicion threshold
Diabetic foot ulcer with cellulitis: code BOTH
Probe-to-bone or MRI for osteomyelitis confirmation
common_misses: Location not specified → L03.90 (no capture)
Necrotizing infection underdiagnosed → MCC lost
Diabetic foot infection without osteo coded
Sepsis from cellulitis not linked
Abscess + cellulitis only one coded
documentation_pearls: If diabetic foot: 'Diabetic foot ulcer, [location], with [cellulitis/osteomyelitis]'
Document organism if cultured (MRSA changes coding and treatment)
If necrotizing concern: document clinical features, imaging, surgical findings

specificity_ladder.label: COVID-19 with pneumonia
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Sequence U07.1 first, J12.82 secondary

specificity_ladder.label: COVID-19 with sepsis (viral)
specificity_ladder.code: U07.1 + A41.89 (or A41.9 if criteria met)
evidence_required: clinical sepsis criteria attributable to COVID

specificity_ladder.label: Personal history of COVID-19
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Use only when no current acute or post-COVID symptoms

specificity_ladder.label: Asymptomatic COVID-19 (incidental positive)
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: Asymptomatic incidental positive = different coding than active disease

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ COVID SPECIFICITY: Confirmed COVID + complications. Code U07.1 first; link complications (pneumonia J12.82, ARDS J80, resp failure J96.0x, sepsis A41.89). Distinguish acute from post-COVID (U09.9) and history (Z86.16).
ai_behavior.rationale: U07.1 sequencing rules and complication linkage drive proper coding. Active vs post-COVID vs history all distinct.

hard_rules: COVID-19 (U07.1) requires confirmed test (PCR, antigen) or physician clinical diagnosis
Sequence U07.1 first when COVID is principal diagnosis
Code each complication separately (pneumonia, ARDS, resp failure, sepsis)
Post-COVID condition (U09.9) requires ≥4 weeks persistence and link to prior infection
Personal history (Z86.16) only when no current active disease
Asymptomatic positive ≠ active disease coding
common_misses: U07.1 not sequenced first when principal
Complications not linked (pneumonia, ARDS, resp failure, VTE)
Long COVID coded as active COVID
Reinfection not distinguished from history
documentation_pearls: If long COVID: '≥4 weeks since acute infection, persistent [symptoms]'
Document treatment: antivirals, steroids, anticoagulation, ventilatory support

Stroke / TIA.base_label: Cerebral infarction / Transient cerebral ischemic attack
Stroke / TIA.cc_mcc: MCC for stroke; CC for TIA

specificity_ladder.label: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of cerebral artery
Cerebral infarction due to embolism
evidence_required: imaging-confirmed infarct + cardioembolic source (AFib, valve, PFO)
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Stroke + AFib documented but cardioembolic mechanism not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Lacunar infarct
evidence_required: small subcortical infarct on imaging, classic small vessel pattern

specificity_ladder.label: Stroke, unspecified
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Always preferable to specify mechanism

specificity_ladder.label: Transient ischemic attack (TIA)
evidence_required: no infarct on imaging (or rapidly resolving)
specificity_ladder.hard_rule: TIA = no imaging evidence of infarct. With infarct = stroke.

specificity_ladder.label: Stroke with NIHSS documentation
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: NIHSS code R29.7xx adds severity; should accompany stroke dx
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Stroke documented without NIHSS — query for NIHSS

specificity_ladder.label: Stroke sequelae (chronic deficits)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Use for late effects, not acute

specificity_ladder.label: Stroke with tPA
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Z92.82 = received tPA in last 24h at outside facility

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ STROKE SPECIFICITY: Specify mechanism (thrombotic, embolic, lacunar, hemorrhagic), location (artery/territory), NIHSS, and tPA status. Cardioembolic stroke + AFib should be linked.
ai_behavior.rationale: Stroke MCC capture depends on specifying mechanism and location. NIHSS adds severity. AFib + stroke = cardioembolic linkage opportunity.

hard_rules: TIA vs stroke: imaging defines (infarct present = stroke)
Mechanism (thrombotic, embolic, lacunar) requires clinical/imaging support
Always document NIHSS at admission
Hemorrhagic stroke distinct from ischemic — different codes
Stroke sequelae (I69.x) ≠ acute stroke
Cardioembolic stroke + AFib should be explicitly linked
common_misses: Mechanism not specified → I63.9 default
NIHSS not documented
AFib + stroke not linked as cardioembolic
tPA receipt not coded with Z92.82
Stroke history coded as acute
documentation_pearls: Always document NIHSS at admission and discharge

specificity_ladder.label: Hypertensive emergency (with end-organ damage)
evidence_required: severe BP elevation (typically >180/120) + acute end-organ damage: encephalopathy, ICH, MI, ACS, pulmonary edema, AKI, dissection, eclampsia
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: BP >180/120 + AMS/MI/AKI/pulmonary edema/dissection but 'hypertensive emergency' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Hypertensive urgency (no end-organ damage)
evidence_required: severe BP elevation without end-organ damage

specificity_ladder.label: Essential hypertension (chronic)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Default chronic HTN code

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ HTN EMERGENCY: Severe BP + end-organ damage = hypertensive emergency (I16.1). Without end-organ damage = urgency (I16.0). Identify and code end-organ damage separately (encephalopathy = MCC, etc.).
ai_behavior.rationale: Emergency vs urgency distinction matters. End-organ damage codes (encephalopathy, MI, AKI) drive MCC capture beyond HTN itself.

hard_rules: Emergency requires end-organ damage; urgency does not
Severe BP alone (>180/120) without symptoms = urgency
Each end-organ damage coded separately
Hypertensive encephalopathy = MCC (I67.4) — links to AMS + BP control response
common_misses: Hypertensive urgency miscoded as emergency
End-organ damage (encephalopathy, MI, AKI) not coded separately
BP elevation without end-organ damage labeled emergency
HTN with HF not coded as I11.0
documentation_pearls: BP values, treatment response, IV vs PO antihypertensives
Code chronic HTN (I10) alongside if applicable

specificity_ladder.label: Septic shock
evidence_required: sepsis + vasopressors despite fluid resuscitation OR persistent hypotension OR lactate >2 post-resuscitation

specificity_ladder.label: Cardiogenic shock
evidence_required: primary cardiac dysfunction (MI, HF, valve, cardiomyopathy, arrhythmia)
hypoperfusion: hypotension, low CI, end-organ hypoperfusion
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: MI/HF + hypotension + vasopressors/inotropes + elevated lactate but 'cardiogenic shock' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Hypovolemic shock
evidence_required: volume loss: hemorrhage, severe dehydration, GI losses

specificity_ladder.label: Obstructive shock (PE, tamponade, tension PTX)
evidence_required: obstruction to cardiac output: massive PE, tamponade, tension pneumothorax
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Massive PE + hypotension OR tamponade + hypotension but obstructive shock not coded

specificity_ladder.label: Mixed shock (e.g., septic + cardiogenic)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Code each shock state when both present

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ SHOCK SPECIFICITY: Hypotension + vasopressors/lactate elevation = shock. Specify type (septic/cardiogenic/hypovolemic/distributive/obstructive). All types = MCC.
ai_behavior.rationale: Shock type identification critical for clinical care and coding. Septic shock requires specific linkage (R65.21). Cardiogenic shock often missed in MI/HF with hypotension.

hard_rules: Hypotension alone ≠ shock without hypoperfusion evidence
Vasopressor requirement is supportive evidence of shock
Septic shock requires sepsis criteria + hemodynamic criteria
Cardiogenic shock requires cardiac etiology + hypoperfusion
Mixed shock states should be coded individually
Differentiate hypovolemic vs hemorrhagic — both R57.1 but document blood loss
common_misses: Septic shock not linked when sepsis + pressors present
Cardiogenic shock missed in MI with hypotension on inotropes
Massive PE + hypotension not coded as obstructive shock
Anaphylactic shock coded as just allergic reaction
Type unspecified → R57.9
documentation_pearls: Document hemodynamics: BP, MAP, lactate, vasopressor doses
End-organ effects: AKI, hepatic dysfunction, AMS, DIC

Pancreatitis.base_label: Acute pancreatitis without necrosis or infection, unspecified

specificity_ladder.label: Acute pancreatitis (mild, interstitial edematous)
evidence_required: clinical/labs/imaging: ≥2 of 3 of (epigastric pain, lipase >3× ULN, imaging consistent)

specificity_ladder.label: Severe acute pancreatitis (with organ failure)
evidence_required: acute pancreatitis + persistent organ failure (>48h): respiratory, cardiovascular, renal
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Pancreatitis + AKI/ARDS/shock but severity not noted

specificity_ladder.label: Chronic pancreatitis
evidence_required: chronic recurrent pancreatitis with structural changes (calcifications, ductal changes)

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ PANCREATITIS SPECIFICITY: Specify etiology (biliary/alcohol/drug/HTG/idiopathic), severity, and complications (necrosis, organ failure). Necrotizing pancreatitis or persistent organ failure = MCC.
ai_behavior.rationale: Etiology and severity drive both clinical care and CDI capture. Necrosis and persistent organ failure are MCC; mild AP is CC.

hard_rules: Diagnosis requires ≥2 of 3 (Atlanta criteria): characteristic pain + lipase >3× ULN + imaging
Lipase elevation alone ≠ pancreatitis (also AKI, post-ERCP without pancreatitis)
Necrosis requires contrast CT confirmation (typically >72h)
Severity: mild (no organ failure, resolves <1 wk), moderate (transient organ failure or local complications), severe (persistent organ failure >48h)
Always identify etiology when possible
common_misses: Etiology not specified → K85.90
Necrosis not coded when present on imaging
Severity not specified
Persistent organ failure (AKI, ARDS) not separately coded
Hypertriglyceridemia not linked
documentation_pearls: State organ failure separately: AKI, ARDS, shock
Imaging findings: edema, necrosis, peripancreatic fluid

specificity_ladder.label: C. difficile enterocolitis, non-recurrent
evidence_required: positive C. diff PCR/EIA + clinical diarrhea

specificity_ladder.label: C. difficile enterocolitis, recurrent
evidence_required: recurrence within 8 weeks of prior episode

specificity_ladder.label: Severe C. difficile (with toxic megacolon)
evidence_required: C. diff + colonic dilation >6 cm + systemic toxicity

specificity_ladder.label: Fulminant C. difficile (with shock or perforation)
evidence_required: C. diff + hypotension/shock OR ileus OR megacolon OR perforation

specificity_ladder.label: C. difficile colitis
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Use specific subtype (.71/.72) when known

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ C. DIFF SPECIFICITY: Specify recurrent vs initial. If severe (megacolon) or fulminant (shock, perforation, ileus) → MCC. Code complications separately.
ai_behavior.rationale: Severe/fulminant C. diff = MCC. Distinguish recurrent (A04.72) from initial (A04.71) for clinical care.

hard_rules: Positive PCR alone in asymptomatic patient ≠ active infection (colonization)
Diarrhea + positive testing = active infection
Severe = WBC >15K, Cr >1.5× baseline, albumin <3, severe abd pain
Fulminant = shock, ileus, megacolon, perforation
Recurrent = within 8 weeks of prior episode
common_misses: Severe/fulminant features present but not coded as MCC
Recurrent vs initial not distinguished
Toxic megacolon, perforation, shock not separately coded
Triggering antibiotics not documented
documentation_pearls: If fulminant: state shock, megacolon, ileus

specificity_ladder.label: Acute blood loss anemia
evidence_required: acute Hgb drop attributable to bleeding
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: GIB/surgery/trauma + Hgb drop but D62 not coded
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Code alongside source of bleeding

specificity_ladder.label: Iron deficiency anemia, due to blood loss (chronic)
evidence_required: microcytic anemia + low ferritin/iron + chronic blood loss

specificity_ladder.label: Anemia of chronic disease (chronic kidney disease)
Anemia in chronic disease classified elsewhere
Hemolytic anemia, autoimmune
evidence_required: positive Coombs, low haptoglobin, elevated LDH/indirect bili, schistocytes

specificity_ladder.label: Severe anemia (Hgb < clinically significant threshold requiring transfusion)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Severity supports clinical care; transfusion requirement noted

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ ANEMIA SPECIFICITY: Specify etiology (acute blood loss, chronic, iron def, CKD, malignancy, hemolytic). Acute blood loss anemia (D62) commonly missed alongside GIB. Anemia in chronic disease (D63.x) links to underlying condition.
ai_behavior.rationale: Unspecified anemia = no capture. Etiology specification supports CC where applicable. D62 with GIB is highest-yield miss.

hard_rules: Acute blood loss anemia (D62) requires documented Hgb drop attributable to acute bleeding
Chronic vs acute distinction matters
Iron deficiency requires labs (ferritin, iron sat) supporting
Anemia of chronic disease requires underlying condition documented
Hemolytic anemia requires lab evidence (LDH, haptoglobin, bilirubin, smear)
common_misses: GIB + Hgb drop but D62 not coded
CKD anemia (D63.1) not linked
Malignancy anemia (D63.0) not linked
Etiology not specified → D64.9
documentation_pearls: Link to source: GIB, CKD, malignancy, deficiency
If acute blood loss: code D62 + bleeding source

Hyperkalemia / electrolyte disturbances.cc_mcc: CC for severe; neither for mild

specificity_ladder.label: Hyperkalemia
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: Severe (K >6.5 or with ECG changes) drives clinical urgency

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ ELECTROLYTE: Specific electrolyte disturbances should be coded individually. Each captures CC. Severity (e.g., K >6.5 with ECG changes) and treatment intensity support clinical accuracy.
ai_behavior.rationale: Each electrolyte disturbance is coded individually. Most = CC. Hyponatremia has its own ladder.

hard_rules: Code each electrolyte disturbance separately
Lab artifact (hemolyzed sample) ≠ true hyperkalemia — repeat needed
Pseudohyperkalemia from tourniquet/hemolysis not coded
Severity matters clinically (treatment intensity)
common_misses: Only one electrolyte coded when multiple present
Lactic acidosis not coded with sepsis
Drug-induced electrolyte disturbance not linked
documentation_pearls: Document trigger (drug, AKI, GI loss, etc.)

specificity_ladder.label: Rhabdomyolysis
evidence_required: context: trauma, immobility, statin, drug, exertion, seizure

specificity_ladder.label: Rhabdomyolysis with AKI
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Rhabdo + Cr rise but AKI not coded
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: AKI in rhabdo often = pigment nephropathy/ATN — see AKI ladder

specificity_ladder.label: Drug-induced rhabdomyolysis
evidence_required: statins, fibrates, colchicine, antipsychotics, illicit drugs

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ RHABDO: Code rhabdomyolysis (M62.82) separately. Always assess for AKI (often pigment nephropathy/ATN — MCC). Identify trigger: drug, trauma, immobility, exertion, seizure, NMS.
ai_behavior.rationale: Rhabdo + AKI is a high-yield combo. AKI from rhabdo is often ATN (MCC). Trigger identification supports clinical care.

hard_rules: CK elevation alone in stable patient may not be rhabdo (consider context)
Rhabdo + AKI: code AKI separately and consider ATN (MCC)
Compartment syndrome is distinct, often surgical emergency
Identify and code trigger (drug, trauma, NMS, malignant hyperthermia)
common_misses: AKI not coded alongside rhabdo
ATN not specified when AKI from pigment nephropathy
Compartment syndrome missed in severe trauma rhabdo
Trigger not identified/coded
documentation_pearls: Document peak CK, Cr trend, urine myoglobin
Link to AKI explicitly when present
State trigger: trauma, immobility, drug, exertion

specificity_ladder.label: Alcohol use disorder, mild/moderate/severe (without withdrawal)
specificity_ladder.documentation_pearl: F10.20 = alcohol dependence (more severe AUD)

specificity_ladder.label: Alcohol withdrawal with perceptual disturbances
evidence_required: withdrawal + visual/auditory/tactile hallucinations with intact reality testing

specificity_ladder.label: Alcohol withdrawal delirium (DTs)
evidence_required: withdrawal + delirium/confusion + hallucinations + autonomic instability + typically 2–4 days after last drink
specificity_ladder.query_trigger: Withdrawal + AMS + autonomic instability + hallucinations but 'DTs' or 'alcohol withdrawal delirium' not stated

specificity_ladder.label: Alcohol withdrawal seizures
evidence_required: withdrawal + seizure activity, typically 6–48h after cessation

specificity_ladder.label: Alcoholic hepatitis
evidence_required: heavy alcohol use + jaundice + transaminitis (AST > ALT) + clinical syndrome

ai_behavior.linkage_flag_template: ⚠ ALCOHOL WITHDRAWAL: Specify severity (uncomplicated vs perceptual disturbances vs delirium tremens). DTs (F10.231) = MCC. Always assess for Wernicke's, alcoholic hepatitis, cirrhosis. Document AUD severity.
ai_behavior.rationale: DTs is MCC and frequently undercoded. Withdrawal severity matters clinically. Comorbid alcoholic liver disease, Wernicke's, malnutrition often missed.

hard_rules: DTs requires delirium + autonomic instability — typically 2–4 days post-cessation
Withdrawal seizures are usually early (6–48h)
CIWA score supports severity but doesn't drive coding
Always assess for Wernicke's (give thiamine before glucose)
Alcoholic hepatitis distinct from cirrhosis (acute vs chronic)
Code AUD (F10.20) alongside withdrawal
common_misses: DTs not specified when delirium + autonomic instability present → MCC lost
Withdrawal seizures not separately coded
Wernicke's missed in malnourished alcoholic with AMS
Alcoholic hepatitis not coded in heavy users
AUD severity not specified
documentation_pearls: Specify withdrawal: uncomplicated, with perceptual disturbances, with delirium (DTs), with seizures
Document CIWA, benzo requirements, ICU need
Always assess: Wernicke's, hepatitis, cirrhosis, malnutrition, pancreatitis

fallback_cc_mcc_table.description: Thin lookup for ICD-10 codes outside the top 32 ladders. AI uses this when a likely diagnosis is identified but no deep ladder exists. Surfaces as soft-flag only.
fallback_cc_mcc_table.format: Reference CMS FY2026 MS-DRG Definitions Manual Appendix C for full CC/MCC list. AI prompt-time reference: when AI encounters a non-laddered diagnosis, it should suggest documentation but not assert specificity beyond what's documented.

E66.01.label: Morbid obesity due to excess calories

ai_behavior.out_of_scope_handling: If AI encounters a diagnosis not in any ladder, it should: (1) suggest the diagnosis if clearly documented, (2) NOT attempt specificity upgrades without ladder support, (3) flag for physician review.

update_log.rationale: CMS FY2027 changes published Aug; review and patch JSON before Oct 1 effective date.
review_checklist: Diff CMS MS-DRG Definitions Manual changes
Review Coding Clinic guidance from prior 4 quarters
